reply_text: ​@violetlavi2207 True, but still, what's the point? All his efforts were in vain. He failed his original goal and failed to avenge those he lost, and instead decided to serve a different purpose. He literally killed himself, just to prove a point to a bunch of greedy people without a soul, and the fact that he says "We are not horses", does not change the fact that more "horses" are, and always will continue to be taken into these games. What does his point change? Everything was in vain and nothing has changed. Sure the Frontman has probably changed, and maybe just maybe there's still humanity and hope within him and tries to do something about it, but we won't know that until (or if) squid game USA comes out.

video_title: Squid Game: The Challenge | Season 2 Official Teaser | Netflix
reply_text: @Sean-hn1vt ...that was as much of a victory as he was ever going to get. He was never going to take down the games or prevent them from spreading - a happy ending where the games are destroyed would've been unrealistic, because greed and capitalism can't be dismantled like that. Gi-hun dying, and for a baby he barely knew at that, was a middle finger to In-ho, the VIPs, and the Games - he can't take them down, but he can still make a statement in his final moments. It's as much of a victory as anyone in his situation can possibly achieve

video_title: Squid Game: The Challenge | Season 2 Official Teaser | Netflix
reply_text:
​@violetlavi2207 Right, I guess that's the heartbreaking truth. But still, literally everything that was building up before this was POINTLESS. What was the point of Gi-hun not getting on the plane? What was the point of that rebellion? What was the point of Jun-ho, spending whole of season 2 searching for the island, only to find it finally at the very end of season 3, just to ask, "why", and then just forget about it all a gew months later and than act like everything is ok? Why would they make so much build-up, and give me a glimmer of hope, just to tell me that "You can't win, you never stood a chance to begin with..." I feel like Gi-hun's last words mean LITERALLY nothing, because all he did was just flip off and prove a point to a bunch of soulless amd permanently immoral individuals, and they don't change anything either, because they've still won. The point doesn't affect anything.

Because, in the end, innocent people are still and will continue, to be brought into these games and bet on for the entertainment of soulless rich people. Because, they are-
